leman||Scrooge.description: Leman presents his credentials to Scrooge.

water-plug||solitude.description: The water-plug's overflowings suddenly turned into misanthropic ice due to its solitude.

Ghost of Jacob Marley||Scrooge.description: The Ghost of Jacob Marley appears suddenly in Scrooge's room, frightening him.

Marley||Ghost of Jacob Marley.description: Jacob Marley transforms into the Ghost of Jacob Marley after death.

Scrooge||Ghost of Jacob Marley.description: Scrooge confronts and questions the Ghost of Jacob Marley about his appearance and purpose.Scrooge was in conflict with the Ghost of Jacob Marley.

Ghost||Scrooge.description: The Ghost initiates a conversation with Scrooge, questioning his disbelief.The Ghost warns Scrooge that he will be visited by Three Spirits.The Ghost guides Scrooge through the house and reveals past memories.The Ghost describes Scrooge's deceased relative as a delicate creature.The Ghost observes Scrooge's emotional reaction to the scene involving Fezziwig and prompts him to reflect on its significance.The Ghost asks Scrooge questions about his state of mind.The Ghost was greatly pleased with Scrooge's mood and looked upon him with favor.

Scrooge||Ghost.description: Scrooge expresses doubt and skepticism towards the existence of the Ghost.The Ghost tells Scrooge about the consequences of his past actions and the need to change.Scrooge observes the Ghost with humility and deference.Scrooge looks at the Ghost and glances anxiously towards the door.Scrooge agrees with the Ghost about his deceased relative and acknowledges its authority.Scrooge speaks to the Ghost about Dick Wilkins.Scrooge observed and engaged with the Ghost in a struggle.Scrooge asks the Ghost if Tiny Tim will live, showing a newfound interest and compassion.Scrooge requested to stay until the guests departed but the Spirit said it could not be done.Scrooge proposed a new game called Yes and No for one half-hour.The Ghost taught Scrooge his precepts.Scrooge interacts with the Ghost, expressing remorse and a desire to change.

Jacob Marley||Scrooge.description: Jacob Marley appears as a ghost to Scrooge, expressing regret for his life choices.
